CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INT PRIMARY KEY, event_date DATE);
SELECT id, event_date, dayofweek(event_date) FROM test_dates;
SELECT id, dayofweek(event_date) FROM test_dates WHERE event_date >= DATE '2020-01-01' AND event_date <= DATE '2023-12-31';
SELECT t1.id, dayofweek(t1.event_date) FROM test_dates t1 JOIN (SELECT id, event_date FROM test_dates WHERE id < 4) t2 ON t1.id = t2.id;
SELECT id, dayofweek(date_trunc('week', event_date)) AS start_of_week FROM test_dates;
SELECT id, dayofweek(event_date) + dayofweek(CURRENT_DATE) AS combined_week_days FROM test_dates;
SELECT dayofweek(event) FROM additional_dates WHERE event IS NOT NULL;
SELECT d.id, dayofweek(d.event_date) FROM test_dates d WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM additional_dates a WHERE dayofweek(a.event) = 2);
